abstract-item1: Every submission must include a structured abstract of no more than 300 words.
abstract-item2: Provide 5 – 8 keywords, separated by semicolons, immediately below the abstract.
camera-ready-intro: Accepted papers must incorporate all required revisions. Camera-ready manuscripts are due by
camera-ready-item1: De-anonymized author block with full names, affiliations, and ORCID identifiers.
camera-ready-item2: A completed copyright-transfer form (provided upon acceptance).
camera-ready-item3: Final PDF compiled from the RMHSTRC 2026 template with embedded fonts.
camera-ready-item4: LaTeX source archive for archival typesetting.
citations-item1-post: package with the author-year citation style.
citations-item2-pre: The bibliography must be generated with BibTeX using the provided
citations-item3: All references must be complete: include authors, title, venue or journal, year, volume, pages, and DOI or URL where available.
citations-item4: Self-citations should not exceed 15% of total references and must be anonymized in the review version.
closing-message: We look forward to your contributions and to an intellectually stimulating program at RMHSTRC 2026.
conference-description: Original contributions spanning AI, computational topology, statistical physics, cognitive science, and game design.
conference-title: 5th Annual RMH Studios Technical Research Conference
contact-intro: For questions regarding the call for papers, please contact the Program Chair:
dates-aoe-note: All deadlines are 23:59 Anywhere on Earth (AoE, UTC-12).
double-blind-intro: RMHSTRC employs a strict double-blind review process. Authors must take care to anonymize their submissions:
double-blind-item1: Remove all author names, affiliations, and acknowledgements.
double-blind-item2: Replace self-references with "[Anonymous]" or refer to prior work in the third person.
double-blind-item3: Do not include links to non-anonymized code repositories or personal websites.
double-blind-item4: Redact funding agency names or grant numbers that could identify the authors.
ethics-p1: All research involving human subjects must have been approved by an IRB or equivalent ethics committee prior to submission.
ethics-p2: Authors must disclose any potential conflicts of interest, sources of funding, and use of generative AI tools in the preparation of the manuscript.
extended-abstracts-desc: Preliminary ideas, position statements, or demonstrations. Extended abstracts are lightly reviewed and presented during the poster session. They are not included in the archival proceedings but are distributed as supplementary material.
figures-item1: Figures must be vector graphics (PDF, EPS) or high-resolution raster images (at least 300 DPI).
figures-item2: All figures and tables must be referenced in the text and captioned descriptively.
figures-item3-pre: Algorithms should be typeset using the
figures-item4: Color figures are permitted but must remain legible when printed in grayscale.
file-format-desc: Submit a single PDF compiled from the official LaTeX template. The PDF must embed all fonts and must not exceed 20 MB for the main manuscript.
format-columns-desc: Two-column format with 0.25 in (6.35 mm) column separation.
format-font-desc: 10 pt Times New Roman (or equivalent serif) for body text. Section headings in 12 pt bold. Mathematics set in Computer Modern or Latin Modern.
format-page-numbers-desc: Omitted in the submission version; added automatically in camera-ready.
format-template-desc: Authors must use the official RMHSTRC 2026 LaTeX template (
format-template-desc2: ), available on the conference website. Microsoft Word submissions are not accepted.
formatting-intro: All submissions must conform to the following specifications. Non-compliant manuscripts will be desk-rejected without review.
full-papers-desc: Original, substantial contributions presenting novel theoretical results, algorithms, empirical studies, or systems. Full papers undergo rigorous double-blind review by at least three members of the Program Committee. Accepted full papers are allocated a 25-minute oral presentation slot followed by 10 minutes of Q&A.
math-item1-pre: All equations must be numbered sequentially using the
math-item2-pre: Definitions, theorems, lemmas, propositions, and corollaries must use the
math-item3-post: and end with a QED symbol.
math-item4: Notation must be consistent throughout the manuscript.
math-typesetting-intro: Given the highly technical nature of RMHSTRC, rigorous mathematical presentation is essential:
overview-p1: The RMH Studios Technical Research Conference (RMHSTRC) is the premier annual venue for original, rigorously peer-reviewed research at the confluence of artificial intelligence, computational mathematics, statistical physics, cognitive science, and interactive-entertainment technology. Now in its fifth year, RMHSTRC 2026 will be held on
overview-p2: We solicit high-quality, previously unpublished manuscripts that advance the state of the art in any of the conference's topical areas. Accepted papers will appear in the
overview-p2-cont: (ISSN 2996-0142) and will be archived in perpetuity through the RMH Studios Digital Library.
research-tagline: Peer-reviewed investigations at the intersection of gaming, artificial intelligence, and cognitive science.
review-intro: Each full paper is assigned to at least three Program Committee members. Reviewers evaluate submissions on:
short-papers-desc: Concise reports of work in progress, negative results of scientific value, or focused case studies. Short papers receive double-blind review by at least two reviewers. Accepted short papers are presented as 5-minute spotlight talks with poster sessions.
submission-method-desc: All submissions must be sent via email to:
submission-subject-intro: Use the following email subject-line format:
submission-types-intro: RMHSTRC 2026 accepts submissions in three categories:
supplementary-item1: Authors may attach supplementary material (proofs, code, datasets, extended results) as a separate PDF or ZIP archive.
supplementary-item2-post: be required to understand the core contribution.
supplementary-item2-pre: Supplementary material is provided for reviewer convenience and
supplementary-item3: Code submissions should include a README with reproduction instructions. Anonymize repository URLs during review.
title-block-item1: The title must be centered, set in 14 pt bold, and should not exceed two lines.
title-block-item2-post: in the review version to maintain double-blind integrity. Replace with "Anonymous Author(s)".
title-block-item2-pre: Author names and affiliations must be
title-block-item3: The camera-ready version must include all author names, institutional affiliations, and corresponding-author email addresses.
topic-adaptive: Adaptive difficulty systems, player modeling, and Bayesian skill estimation
topic-category: Category-theoretic and algebraic approaches to game mechanics
topic-comp-neuro: Computational neuroscience and psychophysiology of play
topic-ethics: Ethics, fairness, and safety in game AI systems
topic-formal: Formal verification and correctness proofs for game logic
topic-generative: Generative models (GANs, diffusion models, VAEs) for game-asset synthesis
topic-hci: Human-computer interaction and UX research in interactive media
topic-hpc: High-performance computing, GPU/TPU optimization for real-time AI
topic-neuro: Neural correlates of flow states, immersion, and cognitive load in gaming
topic-pcg: Procedural content generation and stochastic level design
topic-rl: Reinforcement learning and multi-agent systems in game environments
topic-spectral: Spectral graph theory, Markov-chain methods, and ergodic theory
topic-stat-mech: Non-equilibrium statistical mechanics of learning systems
topic-topology: Computational topology and topological data analysis applied to games or AI
topics-intro: We welcome submissions on — but not limited to — the following topics:
